data.context: Plants grow as they would anywhere else, but specific plant matter and alchemical ingredients have a focus in [The Tribes of Deadreach](../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Tribes%20of%20Deadreach%207a22e355884f421eb813adef3f6bd59d.md).

The many forests of Machora are frequently harvested for lumber, but the forests in [The Sprinwel Theocracy](../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Sprinwel%20Theocracy%20338a8246ef504bb285b34c0055c2bc8e.md) are used for the finest pine and fir lumber and [The Grand Duchy of Sunsadia](../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Grand%20Duchy%20of%20Sunsadia%2032317b81bfc74aaaa8e7be972e2a5676.md) has the decorative ebony.

Common metals are mined throughout the Sixes. There is a mountain in the central (slightly north) part of [The Neverchian Empire](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Neverchian%20Empire%20484c6ba13ac94b688f5b313125fc7ca0.md) where the terraces on the mountain are constantly changing. Within a year, most of the terraces will be different than before. Because of these sudden and tumultuous shifts, not much vegetation grows on the mountain.

There are countless ruins in [The Kingdom of Grashel](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Kingdom%20of%20Grashel%20359225e420c740dfa899f06b3f38e396.md), and nobody knows why. There are abandoned (by the original inhabitants, at least) dungeons, crumbled keeps, standing stones, and other ruins that stretch into much of southern Castleria and some of eastern Mosia.

data.context: **0 BCR/ACR**: The Cirromantic Revelation. People suddenly started finding that their cookbooks, almanacs, and other mundane writing were suddenly magical texts about [Cirromancy](Global/Magic%20Systems/Cirromancy%20d38af43d71564317b166557650a2f262.md). It was discovered that cirromancy was commonly practiced until around 100 BCR, where something dubbed "The Cirromantic Silencing" hid all knowledge of this magic from everyone across The Sixes. The widely accepted theory is that some great cirromancer used a combination of cloudshaped and natural strong sigils to work a devastating Modify Memory spell that kept people from cirromantic knowledge for 100 years.

**44 ACR**: The Great Unmasking. The [The Cabal of Control](Factions/The%20Cabal%20of%20Control%20074e3982dfb64813afc8d28208f2cd82.md) was finally uncovered and overthrown. This shadow organization had infiltrated every government, using enchantment magic to sway leaders across The Sixes. The members of the Cabal were quickly disposed of by assassins and war mages ([The Aegis Coalition](Factions/The%20Aegis%20Coalition%2091e1bc6ae0354d71b3dfa28ab1e139de.md)), but some of the cleverest might have escaped to scheme another day; the Cabal's ultimate intention was never discovered. The obvious security flaw against mind magic led to most rulers hiring magical protection and putting wards around their palaces and manors.

Rholtic ants are a pale species of ant that have evolved to survive and harness the constant noises of the [Plains of Rholtum](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Mosian%20Theocracy/Plains%20of%20Rholtum%20300320b93ccc804887b5da9a66447fe8.md). Their constant neural loop of their brainstem is effectively powered by the periodic sound, so they stop moving without a constant sound. However, this reliance and resistance to the negative effects of the sound also provides them extra energy when they can hear it.

Their attacks are also modified to be claws, teeth, slams, or anything else, and their armor is likely converted to natural armor. Any hard weapon or carapace is a dirty bluish color, being made of [Azurium](../../World%20Info/Materials/Azurium%2018b320b93ccc809cae61c4347f77b247.md). Its skin is gray.

Living Ashstorms haunt [The Bleaklands](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/The%20Bleaklands%2006dba9447b534914ad2b2bc574649dfc.md). When energy of elemental fire was trapped in bubbles in the rock, the stone partially overcame the fire energy and their essences combined.

[Pirate Lord Fjeldar](../../Global/Global%20People/Demigods/Pirate%20Lord%20Fjeldar%202b7320b93ccc80fa8a33c75b0ed98541.md) has the lore.
                
---

***Undying Soul of Fjeldar.*** While attuned to the scimitars, falling unconscious while fighting creatures causes [Pirate Lord Fjeldar](../../Global/Global%20People/Demigods/Pirate%20Lord%20Fjeldar%202b7320b93ccc80fa8a33c75b0ed98541.md) to be summoned. The scimitars slowly rise up, and in three rounds he appears holding the scimitars and rolls initiative, using the stat block on his page. He is not Friendly toward you and your allies, but is Hostile towards your enemies. He remains until slain, and will try to keep the scimitars. He will probably leave to go adventure after he is summoned, until he dies again. Once he dies after being summoned from this feature, he will not be summoned again for 25 years.

The transition into [The Bleaklands](../The%20Bleaklands%2006dba9447b534914ad2b2bc574649dfc.md)’s ecosystem is sharp, but it doesn’t cut to nothing. It sharply cuts to short, unhealthy grass, which fades to ragged shrubs on the rocky floor to the absolute nothing of the majority of The Bleaklands.

There are [Living Ashstorm](../../Mechanics/Monsters/Living%20Ashstorm%20291320b93ccc8044b66bf834fa05bd2b.md)s and occasionally other elementals that roam around. Since it was the site of an ancient volcanic eruption, there is a lot of elemental earth and some elemental fire energy.

There is a covering (20 ft x 20 ft) with a 6 foot tall roof, only posts on the corners. For his undead to take shelter in, in case it rains. There is also a cart in it, which he uses to buy things from the nearest Castlerian town, [Psychraguro (Draft)](../Cities/Psychraguro%20(Draft)%20157320b93ccc805eb572ced7e1f93359.md). He walks a bit carrying it over his shoulders before he’s a safe distance from town to let the skeletons carry it.

A necromancer by trade. The reasons that he settled in the middle of [The Bleaklands](../The%20Bleaklands%2006dba9447b534914ad2b2bc574649dfc.md) are that he found a pre-cataclysm burial site that he uses to get his bodies (for non-skeletons, he gets preserved bodies that were killed by lava when Surtur’s Sundering occurred), and he doesn’t mind being a hermit.

data.id: core:Wresh, Chauntea’s Folly 2b7320b93ccc8060aff7c956fcec4329.md->core:The Brotherhood of Wolf 19134d2b85f94282a2f0e2bb00e777f1.md1
data.context: Wresh is a female dragonborn who secretly leads [The Brotherhood of Wolf](../../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Brotherhood%20of%20Wolf%2019134d2b85f94282a2f0e2bb00e777f1.md). Wresh started as a loyal Nature cleric of Chauntea, was so loyal that Chauntea made an oath to not let Wresh die until her worship was allowed everywhere (there was a nearby elemental cult that was against the gods, they controlled some villages and disallowed worship). Wresh took many years to defeat the hydra-like menace, and eventually succeeded. But in the process, she became more and more corrupt; she disallowed worship of specifically Chauntea to continue her own immortality. To dispel suspicion when ascending to the throne, she outlawed the worship of many gods, only keeping the most obviously benign ones.

data.context:

data.id: core:The Wolf Purist Invasion (Lvl 6) 36b320b93ccc80e291a7efd16c7f3bd5.md->core:Thalia Amakiir f3b7f0332814465b92144d4454fd70c6.md1
data.context: [Thalia Amakiir](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/Cities/Orcholis/Thalia%20Amakiir%20f3b7f0332814465b92144d4454fd70c6.md) quickly gets a briefing on the battle’s progress. Since the main fighting is slow and they are tediously being pushed back into the city, Thalia sends the party to kill the commanding leaders as she goes to command the infantry soldiers. She also sends off a messenger to mobilize more of the army from other parts of the city.

[Spikeback Sally](The%20Road%20to%20Orcholis%20(Lvl%201)/Steal%20from%20Olga%20Frogwart/Spikeback%20Sally%2010a320b93ccc80d788bbe2b5adae36cd.md) is in the middle of arguing with [Olga Frogwart](The%20Road%20to%20Orcholis%20(Lvl%201)/Steal%20from%20Olga%20Frogwart/Olga%20Frogwart%2010a320b93ccc80b5af38c1f8f753719a.md) about the extent of payback for robbing her house. They finally agree that she cannot curse them for life or steal their children, and since her minions have mostly been killed by the party, she attacks and forces them all to make wisdom saves. The person who fails the most turns against the rest of the party, and everyone else who fails is charmed by the hag and cannot hurt her. Her concentration can be broken on these effects. Keep in mind that she can throw a squirrel’s nest from her hair.

[Spikeback Sally](The%20Road%20to%20Orcholis%20(Lvl%201)/Steal%20from%20Olga%20Frogwart/Spikeback%20Sally%2010a320b93ccc80d788bbe2b5adae36cd.md) is in the middle of arguing with [Olga Frogwart](The%20Road%20to%20Orcholis%20(Lvl%201)/Steal%20from%20Olga%20Frogwart/Olga%20Frogwart%2010a320b93ccc80b5af38c1f8f753719a.md) about the extent of payback for robbing her house. They finally agree that she cannot curse them for life or steal their children, and since her minions have mostly been killed by the party, she attacks and forces them all to make wisdom saves. The person who fails the most turns against the rest of the party, and everyone else who fails is charmed by the hag and cannot hurt her. Her concentration can be broken on these effects. Keep in mind that she can throw a squirrel’s nest from her hair.

data.context:
All of you come as recommendations within the city of Goldbreach to [General Kadmos](The%20Road%20to%20Orcholis%20(Lvl%201)/General%20Kadmos%20da8fbcad65c9476ca75abb91d57b378e.md) of Grashel. He needs adventurers to go to Orcholis to deal with a local undead problem. The guards have been fighting off waves of undead almost nightly for the past 2 weeks, and they’ve been getting worse. At first just zombies, but now skeletons and even ghouls. Your job is to investigate the source of these undead and, if you can, stop it. Reward is 100 gp each and further benefits if you end up solving the problem, a smaller portion of that if you just find the source.

[Spikeback Sally](Steal%20from%20Olga%20Frogwart/Spikeback%20Sally%2010a320b93ccc80d788bbe2b5adae36cd.md) stalks the players from the sky on her eagle. Then, she flies down to introduce herself, excusing some light attacks. She tells the characters that she wants an item back from another hag, that the other hag took in an “unfair deal.” She will give them a pot that can hold way more than meets the eye (she demonstrates by shoving her giant eagle into it and taking it out). She invites the characters back into her cabin to to discuss, and tells them that they must take a rust-colored bag and nothing else from [Olga Frogwart’s Tree](Steal%20from%20Olga%20Frogwart/Olga%20Frogwart%E2%80%99s%20Tree%2010a320b93ccc804a8bd6c53ada74f2d4.md). She promises that [Olga Frogwart](Steal%20from%20Olga%20Frogwart/Olga%20Frogwart%2010a320b93ccc80b5af38c1f8f753719a.md) won’t be there, and puts a stipulation into the deal that if Olga shows up, they can get the pot without stealing the bag from Olga.

data.context: > As you approach [Thalia Amakiir](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/Cities/Orcholis/Thalia%20Amakiir%20f3b7f0332814465b92144d4454fd70c6.md)’s office, a few scholars exit. She says, “We just wanted to give you guys an update. We read through all of those documents you found, and some early ones mention a bowl or vial that has some sort of liquid the Cabal is using to create the Remnants. We’ve triangulated the site within a mile, and we have scouts working on tracking it down. The transformation process seems to be quite quick based off of documented communications, and whatever this is is utterly alien. I just started those scholars on researching a way to destroy this “Far Realms Fluid” permanently, so we will likely send for you in the next few days to gather some materials.”

data.id: core:Gather Materials for Destroying Far Realms Fluid ( 301320b93ccc80349f0fdbcd46272048.md->core:Merric Underbough 11f320b93ccc8088a5fdf4388af6551f.md1

data.context: [Merric Underbough](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/The%20Bleaklands/Merric%20Underbough%2011f320b93ccc8088a5fdf4388af6551f.md) has the shards! He was looking for them and lucked upon them, used his basic observatory to predict a dimensional convergence. He's done some experiments with them and has regretted it; therefore, he will give the rest of the shards in exchange for fixing his problem. Since he made a dimensionally traveling skeleton, it left his control as soon as it left the material plane. He needs help to defeat the skeleton, who seems to change every time it phases in.

data.id: core:Gather Materials for Destroying Far Realms Fluid ( 301320b93ccc80349f0fdbcd46272048.md->core:Dimensional Skeleton 31f320b93ccc80b99ef2e1334beff3fe.md1

data.context: A [Dimensional Skeleton](../../Mechanics/Monsters/Dimensional%20Skeleton%2031f320b93ccc80b99ef2e1334beff3fe.md) appears whenever the dimensional shards are disturbed. Defeating the skeleton leaves behind the original skeleton, who is swiftly dispatched by Merric with a rod. The laboratory and walls might be trashed, but Merric thanks the characters nonetheless.

Most of the dungeon is the lair of an echo-shed tyrant ([Echo-Shed Snake](../../../Mechanics/Monsters/Echo-Shed%20Snake%202c2320b93ccc8041b3e3ec2de3152db5.md)). The echoes are strange and unnerving. Materials from throughout the dungeon, such as scrap metals and other resources, can be sold for a total of 500 gp.

data.id: core:Destroy the Far Realms Fluid (Lvl 6) 344320b93ccc801798f4e1d389d26c82.md->core:The Ember 203320b93ccc80888874e3f2521201cf.md1
data.context: Kobolds…… they go in through the hidden entrance no matter what. They’re basically pen testing, sent from [The Ember](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/The%20Brow%20of%20Surtur/The%20Ember%20203320b93ccc80888874e3f2521201cf.md) with permission from the Cabal to confirm that their security is good (since she’s a stakeholder).

data.id: core:Destroy the Far Realms Fluid (Lvl 6) 344320b93ccc801798f4e1d389d26c82.md->core:The Shades eafaf7ae5f444eb88bc21f4e94fa4f38.md1
data.context: A handler (gruff dragonborn Gladiator) comes with his remnant fighting partner, a Displacer Beast with Static Electricity, Many Eyes and Spinal Barbs. The handler is actually a member of [The Shades](../../Factions/The%20Shades%20eafaf7ae5f444eb88bc21f4e94fa4f38.md), and is an elite mercenary. He is looking to sell Cabal information on the black market. Since the pen testing is currently going on, he is looking to use the chaos to gain this kind of information.
He has a sick [Azurium](../../World%20Info/Materials/Azurium%2018b320b93ccc809cae61c4347f77b247.md) spear and shield.

If the dwarf is killed without the fluid being taken, it will spill and transform her. If the fluid is destroyed, she will scream “NOOOO!” and try to desperately pour it over herself, causing the same transformation. The dwarf then uses the Fomorian stat block, but replace its languages with only Common. Give it the [Remnants](../../Mechanics/Monsters/Remnants%2015b320b93ccc806d864de7271b7dab92.md) features Squishy Flesh, Flesh of Ooze, and Scything Arms

data.id: core:Delay the Cabal’s Scheme (Lvl 7) 366320b93ccc8051a6ebf6210b9dfa1b.md->core:The Elemental Council of Three 80615ab953f44489889372b97ef419a4.md1
data.context:
Raze ([The Elemental Council of Three](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/Cities/Orcholis/The%20Elemental%20Council%20of%20Three%2080615ab953f44489889372b97ef419a4.md)) also joins the fight.
                
---

As you reenter the Tangled Hydra, [The Elemental Council of Three](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/Cities/Orcholis/The%20Elemental%20Council%20of%20Three%2080615ab953f44489889372b97ef419a4.md) is all present at once, and they have a new member! The kobold they saved (named Ets or Edz) is happy to see the characters, and unhappy that she’s supposed to act very grim. Apparently she’s temporarily filling a fourth (newly added) position, and the real 4th member arrives later (F Azer Sentinel).

After the fight, Raze angrily says that this is unacceptable and rights a table that his wind pushed over, then goes back to brooding at the bar. Medrash will pay the characters for saving him, give them a token of goodwill from The Shades, or give them the Cabal information that he was nearly killed for (obviously they choose that one). He was found out by the Cabal when he intercepted (and kept) the message to secure a [Demonicon (Basic)](../../Mechanics/Items/Demonicon%20(Basic)%20366320b93ccc8087a3a2c73c9537826c.md) by any means possible. This means that there is a bit of time, and the characters better get on securing all nearby Demonicons.
                
---

The archivist luminary (head librarian) of Orcohlis’s Luminary Library is Alekto Ramondo (M human, helpful but absentminded). When asked about the [Demonicon (Basic)](../../Mechanics/Items/Demonicon%20(Basic)%20366320b93ccc8087a3a2c73c9537826c.md), he will lightly insist on calling it a “Class II Abyssal Compendium.” (Nonmagical is class I, higher classes exist.) After a quick vetting process that includes reading a message from Thalia and asking if they’re evil, he leads the players to a locked up section with dangerous books, and brings it back to them. He allows them to borrow it for three weeks.

[Nyx Leto](../../Map%20and%20Ecosystems/Cities/Orcholis/Nyx%20Leto%2026b320b93ccc804ab455e58e09b84c3e.md). A male noble in green and an ostentatious floral hat and a female in red and white with a cane, all similar style to Nyx’s. Nyx doesn’t actually have much money, and is only putting in bids when he knows he won’t have to pay them.

This encounter occurs after the first book is found. Two cabal Assassins and a Mage with Silence and 7 uses of Subtle Spell attack at an opportune time, when the characters are hemmed in. However, [Calder](../../../Campaign%20B/Characters/Mira/Calder%202a4320b93ccc803a9410c58b8b867165.md) randomly shows up to defend (as this encounter is absolutely stacked).

Used to be a mercenary in the East Sixes, part of a mercenary company (priestly order dedicated to Tempus, the god of war). Hired by [The Duchy of Nemouth](../../Machora/Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Duchy%20of%20Nemouth%2019520e51eeb04aecad612f8590a81a02.md) to defend against aggression from [The Falian Empire](../../Machora/Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Falian%20Empire%20e8596c01e6c0482791243e1265b58079.md), but the Cabal saw it in their interest to disband the mercenary organization to stop defense of that territory. Used Enchantment to infiltrate the company command structure, climactic battle and their company got recked. Abandoned the contract after they got decimated, leader dispersed them throughout the world to hunt Cabal members in retribution, not to return until they’re all snuffed out.

4. Hugh remembers a man with a dog instructing him to follow Demetrius, he has been subliminally following that. [Akamas, Son of Thesius](Characters/Demetrius%20(Amelia)/Akamas,%20Son%20of%20Thesius%20120320b93ccc80108316d39e7ef1a4c0.md).
- Her and the other miners were hired at a very high price from [Psychraguro (Draft)](../../../Machora/Map%20and%20Ecosystems/Cities/Psychraguro%20(Draft)%20157320b93ccc805eb572ced7e1f93359.md).

- Helja was suspicious of it from the start, as no one had ever mined in [The Bleaklands](../../../Machora/Map%20and%20Ecosystems/The%20Bleaklands%2006dba9447b534914ad2b2bc574649dfc.md). The people only had newly purchased equipment, and no one who hired the miners knew a thing about mining.

A soldier in army of [The Kingdom of Grashel](../../Machora/Map%20and%20Ecosystems/States/The%20Kingdom%20of%20Grashel%20359225e420c740dfa899f06b3f38e396.md), stationed in his hometown of [Griesberg (Draft)](../../Machora/Map%20and%20Ecosystems/Cities/Griesberg%20(Draft)%20199320b93ccc80d3a736c4041be74171.md) to lead the local militia. A colonel within the military for most of his service, although he was technically demoted to major after the defense of his hometown.
